title: Travel Disruptions Reported Across Rochdale Network
excerpt: Multiple transport services in Rochdale are experiencing delays and disruptions due to ongoing issues and maintenance work.
content_html:
Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) has issued alerts regarding disruptions affecting various services in the Rochdale area. These changes are expected to impact travel plans for many commuters and residents.

As of 8 July, delays are reported on the Altrincham and Manchester Airport lines due to a points fault at Market Street. Passengers are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the situation develops.

Additionally, the lift at Central Park tram stop, which serves the route towards Rochdale via Oldham, is currently out of service. Travellers needing step-free access are directed to use the lift from The Gateway to the Manchester bound platform and then cross the tracks to reach the Oldham bound platform.

Essential improvement works are also scheduled at Prestwich Metrolink stop from 18 May to 19 August. While tram services will not be affected, there may be an increased presence of staff at this location during the works.

Passengers are encouraged to check for updates on the TfGM travel alerts page to stay informed about any further changes or disruptions to services.

title: Getting to Number One Riverside: Your Guide to Accessing Council Services
excerpt: Number One Riverside, the Rochdale Borough Council offices, is easily accessible by public transport and offers nearby parking options.
content_html:
Number One Riverside serves as the main council office for Rochdale Borough Council, located on Smith Street. This multi-use public building is designed to accommodate various council services and community needs.

For those planning to visit, Number One Riverside is conveniently situated just a minute's walk from Rochdale Interchange and the Rochdale Town Centre Bee Network stop. Additionally, Rochdale Train Station is a 14-minute walk away, with tram services available to reach the town centre quickly.

Visitors can find car parking options within a short walking distance from the building. There are also designated spaces for disabled parking, where blue badge holders can benefit from free parking at several council car parks.

The opening times for Number One Riverside align with those of Rochdale Central Library and the Customer Service Centre, ensuring that residents have ample opportunity to access council services throughout the week.
